description: lib.rs:41 uses owned PathBuf, then lib.rs:76 and lib.rs:110 clone() it for every spawn_blocking closure. PathBuf is the right type for ONE owner (a builder) but FileBackend exposes the path via async methods that move into closures; that pattern wants Arc<Path>. Idiomatic shape: 'path: Arc<Path>' field, 'Arc::clone(&self.path)' move into closure (refcount bump, no heap alloc). This is the same idiom as Tokio's File or Hyper's Uri internal storage. Cross-references qth2.9 (efficiency) — same fix, both lenses.
title: FileBackend::path: PathBuf forces clone on every async call; Arc<Path> is the idiomatic shared-readonly form
